title: CMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS set to MSVCRTD.lib (Debug CRT) while CMAKE_MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY requests MultiThreaded (Release CRT)
description: Line 65 sets CMAKE_MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY to 'MultiThreaded' (the static release CRT), but line 67 sets CMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS to 'MSVCRTD.lib', which is the static *debug* CRT. The comment says this is only for cmake's compile-time CRT probe executables, but mixing release and debug CRTs in probe executables can produce confusing cmake check failures on some Windows SDK configurations. The correct probe library to pair with MultiThreaded is 'libcmt.lib' or 'MSVCRT.lib', not 'MSVCRTD.lib'.

title: build.rs only re-runs when wrapper.h or DEP_JXL_PATH changes, not when the libjxl C headers themselves change
description: The rerun-if-changed directives cover wrapper.h and the DEP_JXL_PATH env var, but not the actual C header files that wrapper.h includes (e.g. external/libjxl/lib/include/jxl/decode.h, encode.h, etc.). If a developer updates libjxl headers in-place without changing DEP_JXL_PATH, cargo will not re-run bindgen and the generated bindings will be stale. The fix is to emit 'cargo:rerun-if-changed=<include_dir>' or to add a glob over the jxl/*.h files after computing the include path.

title: lib_dir fallback to lib64 silently succeeds even if lib64 also does not exist, producing a non-fatal broken link-search path
description: If neither 'lib' nor 'lib64' exists under the cmake prefix (e.g. cmake installed to 'lib/x86_64-linux-gnu' on some Debian/Ubuntu systems), the fallback returns the non-existent lib64 path and emits it as a rustc-link-search without error. Subsequent cargo:rustc-link-lib directives will produce linker errors that are hard to diagnose. An assertion or a check for a third common layout ('lib/x86_64-linux-gnu') would surface the problem earlier.

title: cfg!(windows) checks the build-script host OS, not the cargo target, so cross-compiling to Windows from Linux skips all MSVC/ClangCL cmake flags
description: build.rs runs on the host machine. cfg!(windows) is true only when the host is Windows. If someone cross-compiles this crate targeting Windows from a Linux/macOS CI host, the ClangCL generator, /Zl, and /O2 flags are all omitted, and cmake will attempt to use the default generator (Ninja/Make + GCC/clang) against a Windows target — producing a mis-linked or broken libjxl. The correct guard is target.contains("windows") (the TARGET env var already captured at line 22) rather than cfg!(windows).

title: Smoke test only checks v > 0; it does not verify the linked libjxl matches the expected major.minor version
description: JxlDecoderVersion() returns major*1000000 + minor*1000 + patch. The test asserts v > 0, which passes for any version including unintended downgrades (e.g. a system libjxl 0.7 sneaking in instead of the in-repo 0.11). A tighter assertion such as v >= 11_000_000 (= 0.11.0) or matching the expected semver from Cargo.toml would catch version mismatches at test time rather than at runtime when codec behavior diverges.
